required_skills: Guidance, navigation, and control (GNC) algorithm design and implementation
State estimation and sensor fusion using Kalman filtering (EKF, UKF, particle filters)
Attitude determination and control system (ADCS) design for spacecraft
Orbital mechanics and trajectory design including maneuver planning and delta-V budgeting
Control theory including PID, LQR, H-infinity, and model predictive control (MPC)
Six-degree-of-freedom (6-DOF) vehicle dynamics modeling and simulation
Inertial navigation system (INS) integration with GPS and star tracker sensor suites
MATLAB and Simulink for algorithm development, simulation, and Monte Carlo analysis
Flight dynamics analysis including stability, controllability, and robustness margins
preferred_skills: Experience with autonomous rendezvous and proximity operations (RPO) or docking algorithms
Knowledge of terrain-relative navigation (TRN) and optical navigation techniques
Familiarity with machine learning-based perception for autonomous GNC applications
Experience with entry, descent, and landing (EDL) systems design
Multi-target tracking and data association algorithms
Experience with precision landing or terminal guidance for launch vehicles
Familiarity with NASA GMAT or JPL Monte for trajectory optimization
responsibilities: Design, implement, and verify GNC algorithms including attitude estimation, orbit determination, and trajectory guidance for launch vehicles or spacecraft
Develop extended Kalman filter (EKF) and unscented Kalman filter (UKF) state estimators for real-time sensor fusion of IMU, GPS, star tracker, and LIDAR data
Build high-fidelity 6-DOF vehicle dynamics models in MATLAB/Simulink incorporating aerodynamics, propulsion, and flexible body effects for Monte Carlo dispersion analysis
Design attitude determination and control systems (ADCS) including reaction wheel, magnetorquer, and thruster-based actuator selection and control law development
Implement and validate trajectory optimization algorithms for orbital transfer, station-keeping, rendezvous, and precision landing maneuvers
Execute hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) testing using dSPACE to validate GNC flight software against simulated sensor inputs and actuator dynamics
Perform stability and robustness analysis of closed-loop control systems using frequency-domain methods to ensure adequate gain and phase margins
Collaborate with flight software engineers to integrate GNC algorithms into the onboard software stack and validate timing, throughput, and numerical precision
Define navigation sensor requirements and interface control documents for IMU, GPS, star tracker, and optical sensors used in the vehicle navigation architecture
Support mission operations and anomaly resolution by analyzing telemetry data to assess GNC system performance and diagnose off-nominal attitude or navigation events
